assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is denominated in a foreign currency and is a monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
Any hedge designation would otherwise meet IFRS 9 eligibility, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable can be considered for a fair value hedge of its foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements if the IFRS 9 6.3.6 exception is met.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised asset or liability may also be designated in a cash flow hedge for foreign currency-related variability in cash flows, making this a distinct hedge accounting model to consider.
